checks: When the ball falls off the track or hits an obstacle, a 'GAME OVER' screen/overlay appears in the center of the screen instead of the game immediately resetting
The game-over screen displays the player's final score (e.g., 'Score: 3')
If the score qualifies for the top 10, a text input field for entering a name is visible on the game-over screen
A submit button is visible next to or below the name input field
After submitting a name, the game resets and a new run begins — the ball is back at the start position, score is 0, and obstacles are regenerated
A 'Leaderboard' button is visible in the top-right area of the screen during gameplay
Clicking the Leaderboard button opens a panel/overlay showing the top high scores
The leaderboard displays each entry with a rank number (#1, #2, etc.), player name, and score, sorted from highest to lowest score
A Close button on the leaderboard panel dismisses it and returns to gameplay view
After submitting a high score and resetting, clicking the Leaderboard button shows the newly submitted score in the list
Opening a second browser tab/window and submitting a score in one tab causes the other tab to see that score when opening the leaderboard (scores are shared globally via the backend API)
Sending a direct API request via curl 'curl http://localhost:8080/api/scores' returns a JSON array of scores

Sending a POST to the API with a valid score via curl returns the updated leaderboard: curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/scores -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"CurlTest","score":99}'
Sending an invalid POST (negative score) returns a 400 error response: curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/scores -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"Bad","score":-1}'
The game still functions normally: ball rolls forward, moves laterally with head tilt, coins can be collected and increment the score, obstacles trigger falling
No visual artifacts or overlapping UI elements — the game-over overlay, leaderboard panel, and gameplay UI are cleanly separated
